meta.title: F46 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 engine — B47, B48, B38, B37. Real UK costs, reliability data and common failures. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

@graph.name: BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 Engine Codes
BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 Core Variants

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned F46 engine typically costs £1,800–£5,500 supply only, depending on engine — the B47 diesel sits at £3,200–£5,500, while the B38 petrol runs £1,800–£3,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B47 diesel (218d/220d) — it's a significant improvement over the notorious N47, with the timing chain relocated to the front of the engine, making it both more durable and easier to service. With proper maintenance, it's the most dependable long-term option in the F46 range.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46?

acceptedAnswer.text: B47 diesel EGR cooler and valve issues, B47/B48 timing chain wear at very high mileage, B48 petrol cooling system leaks, and B38/B37 three-cylinder carbon build-up on intake valves. Regular maintenance and long runs help prevent many of these issues.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B-series engines (B38, B37, B48, B47) are widely regarded as a significant reliability improvement over the earlier N-series engines. The B47 diesel is particularly robust, with the timing chain relocated to the front of the engine. Regular maintenance and BMW-approved oil are essential for long-term reliability.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but early B47 diesels (2015–2018) may have EGR cooler issues that BMW addressed with later revisions. A B47 with full service history and documented EGR work is a safe bet. The B48 petrol is also a solid choice if you prefer petrol.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my F46 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start rattle (B47/B48 timing chain), engine warning lights (EGR issues), coolant loss (B48 cooling system), rough idle (carbon build-up), or blue smoke and loss of power are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well‑kept £12,000 F46, a £4,000 B47 recon is ~33% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £6,000 example, the same recon brushes our 70% BMW threshold — walk away. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B47 diesel can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing — the front-mounted timing chain is a significant durability improvement. The B48 petrol is similarly durable. The B38 and B37 three-cylinder units are also robust if carbon build-up is managed with regular longer runs.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the F46 Gran Tourer and the F45 Active Tourer?

acceptedAnswer.text: The F46 Gran Tourer (this page) is the seven-seat MPV variant with a 110 mm longer wheelbase, third-row seating, and a more practical interior. The F45 Active Tourer is the five-seat MPV with a shorter wheelbase and smaller boot. Both share the same UKL1 platform and engine range — see the F45 Generation Page for that model's specifics.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below. Tell us your variant (216i, 218i, 220i, 214d, 216d, 218d, 220d) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every petrol and diesel engine fitted to the seven-seat F46 Gran Tourer — from the entry-level three-cylinder to the range-topping 2.0-litre units.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All reliability ratings are [PROVISIONAL] — no dedicated Engine Page exists yet for any F46 engine family. However, the B-series engines (B38, B37, B48, B47) are widely regarded as a significant reliability improvement over the earlier N-series engines. The B47 relocated the timing chain to the front of the engine, making it both more durable and easier to service compared to the notorious N47. Ratings are derived from cross-referencing multiple specialist and owner sources.

overview.h2: BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 — Overview
overview.intro: The F46 is the seven-seat MPV variant of BMW's 2 Series, launched in June 2015 as a longer, more versatile version of the F45 Active Tourer. It was BMW's first and only three-row MPV, built on a front-wheel-drive platform shared with the MINI brand. The wheelbase was extended by 110 mm (4.3 inches) over the Active Tourer to accommodate the third row. The engine range initially featured two petrol units (218i, 220i) and three diesel units (216d, 218d, 220d xDrive). A significant 2018 update brought styling tweaks and the option of a new 7-speed automatic gearbox. Production ended in late 2021 with no direct successor.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW UKL1 (front-wheel-drive, shared with MINI) • Predecessor: None (BMW's first seven-seat MPV) • Successor: None (discontinued in 2021) • Sibling body styles in this generation: F45 Active Tourer (five-seat MPV)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The F46 Gran Tourer generated 256 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the B47 diesel units as these MPVs reach higher mileages and owners seek reliable replacement options.

items.quote: The B47 is BMW's modular four-cylinder diesel that replaced the notorious N47. It relocated the timing chain to the front of the engine, making it both more durable and easier to service. With 136–190 bhp and strong torque, it delivers excellent economy (50–60 MPG) and proven reliability. It's the engine the F46 was built for.
items.whoItsFor: Buyers who need diesel economy, torque and the best long-term reliability.

items.quote: The 2.0-litre B48 delivers 163–192 bhp with smooth, refined power delivery. It's BMW's modular four-cylinder petrol engine, sharing the B-series family's improved reliability over earlier turbocharged BMW engines. It's the pick for buyers who don't want diesel.
items.whoItsFor: Petrol buyers who want smooth power without diesel complexity.

items.quote: While the B47 is a significant improvement over the N47, early examples can still suffer from timing-chain wear at very high mileage. EGR valve and cooler failures are also documented. Later post-2018 cars with the revised B47TÜ1 are the safest bet.
items.whoItsFor: Buyers who understand the risks and maintain their cars properly.

items.engine: B47 Diesel Timing Chain / Turbo Failure
items.quote: Although the B47's timing chain is more durable than the N47's, a chain failure at high mileage still means a full engine replacement. Turbocharger wear from neglected oil changes is another costly failure point. A full B47 replacement can exceed £5,500.
items.whoItsFor: Buyers who budget for preventative maintenance.

items.quote: The 1.5-litre three-cylinder B38 offers 136 bhp and genuine 40+ MPG economy in a family MPV. It's the entry point to the F46 range but punches above its weight — smooth, characterful, and more reliable than the diesels' emission-system complexity.
items.whoItsFor: Buyers who want the lowest running costs and don't need diesel torque.

items.quote: The 1.5-litre three-cylinder diesel is the most efficient engine in the F46 range — 116 bhp and genuine 60+ MPG. It's often overlooked in favour of the 2.0-litre diesels, but for urban or light-use buyers, it's a genuine alternative.
items.whoItsFor: Urban buyers who want maximum economy in a seven-seat package.

ownershipEconomics.h2: BMW 2 Series Gran Tourer F46 — Ownership Economics

rows.ownershipVerdict: Solid Choice — simple three-cylinder, cheap to run

rows.commonMajorFailure: Oil filter housing leaks, coolant system

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Economy — three-cylinder diesel, excellent MPG

rows.commonMajorFailure: Timing chain wear (high mileage), EGR cooler, turbo
rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Diesel — the pick of the range, proven reliability

economicsRule.title: The F46 rule
economicsRule.text: A £3,200–£5,500 B47 recon against a £6,000–£15,000 Gran Tourer sits at 21–92% of the car's value. On a sub-£7,000 high-mileage example, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £12,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 27–46% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first, and check service history carefully.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F46 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 218i (B38) — simple three-cylinder petrol, cheap parts, no DPF.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F46 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 220d (B47) — diesel complexity, EGR/DPF/turbo costs, but the best all-rounder.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F46 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 218d (B47) with full service history — strong economy, proven reliability, most common variant.

problems.title: B47 Diesel EGR Cooler & Valve Failures
problems.rootCause: The B47's EGR (exhaust gas recirculation) cooler can develop leaks or become clogged with soot, leading to coolant loss, poor running, and in some cases, engine warning lights. BMW has issued recalls on some B47 vehicles for EGR cooler issues. The EGR valve itself can also become stuck with carbon deposits.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of reduced performance or an engine warning light, have the EGR system inspected. A £350–£700 cleaning or service is dramatically cheaper than the £800+ replacement that follows a seized valve or cracked cooler. Regular long drives help prevent DPF and EGR issues.

cta.label: Compare B47 diesel EGR repair prices for your F46 →

problems.title: B47 / B48 Timing Chain Wear (High Mileage)
problems.rootCause: While the B47 and B48 engines relocated the timing chain to the front of the engine — a significant improvement over the N47's rear-mounted design — high-mileage examples can still experience chain stretch. Some B47 engines may not have received all updated timing chain components from the factory. Cold-start rattle is the classic early warning.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold-start rattle or a "Drivetrain" warning, have the timing chain inspected immediately. A £800–£1,400 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,200+ replacement that follows if ignored. Regular oil changes with BMW-approved oil are essential.

cta.label: Compare B47 engine prices for your F46 →

problems.title: B48 Petrol Cooling System Leaks
problems.rootCause: The B48 petrol engine is known for cooling system issues — the water pump, thermostat housing, and oil filter housing can develop coolant leaks. The plastic coolant pipes can become brittle and crack, especially at the connection points. This is a well-documented BMW B-series engine weakness.
problems.recommendation: Check coolant levels regularly and inspect for any signs of leaks around the water pump and oil filter housing. A £250–£550 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £1,800+ replacement that follows an overheat.

cta.label: Compare B48 engine prices for your F46 220i →

problems.title: B38 / B37 Three-Cylinder Carbon Build-up
problems.rootCause: Direct injection engines like the B38 and B37 are prone to carbon build-up on intake valves. Short journeys and urban driving make the problem worse. Symptoms include rough idle, reduced power, and increased fuel consumption.
problems.recommendation: Use premium fuel and take the car on regular longer runs to help burn off carbon deposits. A £200–£400 professional cleaning is far cheaper than the £1,800+ replacement that follows neglected carbon build-up.

cta.label: Compare B38 engine prices for your F46 218i →
